title: Trichosalpinx tenera
taxonomy: Trichosalpinx tenera is a species of orchid, specifically classified within the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. This subtribe is part of the large family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it belongs to the genus Trichosalpinx. The species is native to Central America. Trichosalpinx tenera is considered a terrestrial orchid.

intro: Trichosalpinx tenera is a species of epiphytic orchid, commonly found in Central and South America, characterized by its delicate white flowers and slender stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Trichosalpinx tenera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Trichosalpinx tenera is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in traditional medicine formulations such as salves and ointments.
